response:
Short answer: we follow an edge‑to‑backend, logs‑first troubleshooting pattern that identifies whether App Gateway/WAF is blocking traffic, finds the exact WAF rule/RuleId and request field, correlates with backend/App Service health, applies the least‑privilege WAF fix (allow short‑circuit, IP allowlist or targeted per‑rule exclusion), and then validate.

Typical steps we use on fazure‑mcp

- Start at the edge: pull AGWAccessLogs and AGWFirewallLogs for the time window to see status codes, client IPs, timestamps and the exact WAF matches (RuleId, matched field, RequestUri including query string).
- Correlate with backend: check App Gateway backend health, probe config, App Service state (azure-mcp-prod), App Insights and App Service metrics (Requests, Http5xx, HealthyHostCount). This tells you if the backend returned 500s or the gateway/WAF is blocking.
- Identify root cause in WAF: read the AGWFirewallLogs to find which custom rule or managed rule IDs fired (examples seen: 920230, 942xxx family, 931130, 949110) and which request component (e.g., ARGS:params.arguments.query, redirect_uri) triggered the block.
- Apply the minimum safe remediation:
  - If trusted internal traffic is being blocked, add a short‑circuit allow rule (per‑listener or global) that matches Host + RequestUri (include query string), client IP ranges (Rackspace VPN or Janus AWS), and give it a higher priority than blockers. Example: AllowTrustedVpnFazureMcp at priority 3 after RateLimit=1 and VpnOnly=2.
  - If specific managed rule false positives occur, add a targeted per‑rule exclusion (narrow field exclusion) for the JSON arg instead of disabling whole rule sets.
  - If missing trusted IPs, update IPMatch lists (e.g., add the four Janus AWS IPs).
- Re‑test and validate: replay the failing requests (or run burst tests), confirm AGW logs no longer show the block RuleId, watch App Gateway metrics and App Insights, and confirm expected response codes. Verify RateLimit behaviour if relevant.
- Iterate: refine regexes to account for query strings, ensure allow rules are attached in the correct policy scope (global vs site), and confirm WAF rule ordering/priority.

This is the repeatable “edge logs → correlate backend → identify WAF rule/field → minimal targeted fix (allow or exclusion/IP) → validate” pattern we follow for fazure‑mcp.

{"ns": "azure-msp", "task": "debug", "facts": ["tri-auth pattern → implements → APIM policy XML", "tri-auth pattern → depends_on → Azure AD app registration", "tri-auth pattern → uses → DDI tenant isolation headers", "tri-auth pattern → contains → subscription key", "tri-auth pattern → uses → APIM authentication debugging pattern", "tri-auth pattern → depends_on → XML", "tri-auth pattern → contains → XML", "APIM authentication debugging pattern → targets → APIM", "APIM authentication debugging pattern → used_by → auth debugging sessions", "APIM authentication debugging pattern → integrates_with → agent-fleet-api", "APIM authentication debugging pattern → uses → APIM on-error policy"], "recent": ["2026-04-15: On 2026-04-15, investigation of fazure-mcp 500/502/403 traffic found that the current issue was not WAF regression and not backend 500s. In AGWAccessLogs for fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com over the prior 24h, there were 0 HTTP 500 responses, multiple 502s concentrated around 13:46-13:50 UTC and 14:14-14:15 UTC and especially 14:38-14:51 UTC, and a small number of 403s. AGWFirewallLogs showed trusted Rackspace/Janus traffic matching AllowTrustedVpnFazureMcp and current 403s were mainly expected VpnOnly blocks from external/scanner or preview IPs such as 146.70.189.237 and 52.123.190.89/125. The fazure-mcp backend is App Service azure-mcp-prod in rg-azure-mcp-prod with linked App Insights azure-mcp-prod. App Service state was Running and App Gateway show-backend-health reported the backend healthy. App Service metrics showed Requests and Http2xx continuing through the window and Http5xx stayed 0 throughout, confirming the backend was not returning server 500s. App Gateway metrics showed HealthyHostCount stayed 1 and UnhealthyHostCount stayed 0 throughout the incident while FailedRequests spiked, indicating short transient backend unavailability that did not trip health-state change
[specifics: app_service: azure-mcp-prod (rg-azure-mcp-prod); app_insights: azure-mcp-prod; probe_config: host=azure-mcp-prod.prodengineeringase.p.azurewebsites.net; path=/health; interval=300s; timeout=30s; unhealthyThreshold=3; other_probes_interval: 30s; backend_http_setting: fazure-mcp-backend; protocol=HTTPS; port=443; requestTimeout=20s; workers: numberOfWorkers=1; health_counts: HealthyHostCount=1; UnhealthyHostCount=0; http5xx: Http5xx=0; manual_restart: daniel.temalatzi@rackspace.com from 72.32.180.178; cert_issue: managed certificate renewal failed because DNS pointed to App Gateway IP=20.118.70.227 instead of App Service IP]", "2026-04-14: On 2026-04-14, after new MCP 403s from Janus AWS IPs, added the full Janus AWS address set 52.39.2.74, 52.41.136.221, 52.10.115.132, and 52.40.141.99 to the trusted IP allowlists for fazure MCP and oauth endpoints on Azure Application Gateway. Root cause was that recent POST /mcp failures on fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com at 2026-04-14T17:51:41Z through 2026-04-14T18:06:13Z were blocked by custom rule VpnOnly under policy scope fazure-mcp, even though those IPs are the known JanusAWS set already present in the global policy. Fixed live by updating site policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites rules AllowTrustedVpnFazureMcp and VpnOnly, and global policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax rules AllowTrustedVpnFazureOauthMcpAuth and FazureOauthVpnOnly, adding all four Janus AWS IPs to the IPMatch lists.
[specifics: janus_aws_ips: 52.39.2.74, 52.41.136.221, 52.10.115.132, 52.40.141.99; fazure_mcp_hostname: fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com; blocked_method_and_path: POST /mcp; blocked_rule: VpnOnly (policy_scope=fazure-mcp); site_policy: waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites; site_policy_rules_updated: AllowTrustedVpnFazureMcp; VpnOnly; global_policy: waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax; global_policy_rules_updated: AllowTrustedVpnFazureOauthMcpAuth; FazureOauthVpnOnly; ipmatch_update: added all four Janus AWS IPs to IPMatch lists of the above rules]", "2026-03-25: On 2026-03-25, investigated fazure-mcp WAF 403s for POST /mcp from Rackspace VPN IP 72.32.180.181. Local Azure-MCP rollout artifacts showed the per-listener WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites was created by copying managedRules and policySettings from the global policy, then attaching only site custom rules RateLimit, VpnOnly, BlockSensitiveFiles, BlockKnownPath, GeoBlocking, and AllowSafeFilters. The global RackspaceVPN Allow rule was not copied into the site policy. Result: trusted VPN traffic still traverses OWASP CRS 3.2 managed rules on /mcp and legitimate MCP SQL request bodies are likely blocked as SQLi false positives. Recommended fix is either a listener-policy custom Allow rule for trusted VPN /mcp traffic or a narrower per-rule exclusion on the JSON arg carrying SQL text after confirming the exact RuleId in AGWFirewallLogs.
[specifics: waf_policy: waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites; attached_custom_rules: RateLimit, VpnOnly, BlockSensitiveFiles, BlockKnownPath, GeoBlocking, AllowSafeFilters; missing_rule: RackspaceVPN Allow; managed_rules_version: OWASP CRS 3.2; source_ip: 72.32.180.181; endpoint: POST /mcp; likely_blocked: legitimate MCP SQL request bodies due to SQLi false positives; recommended_fix_allow: listener-policy custom Allow rule for trusted VPN /mcp traffic; recommended_fix_exclusion: narrower per-rule exclusion on JSON arg carrying SQL text (confirm RuleId in AGWFirewallLogs); log_source: AGWFirewallLogs]", "2026-03-25: On 2026-03-25, a reusable WAF mitigation pattern was established for trusted internal MCP SQL traffic behind Azure Application Gateway. Rackspace VPN IP 72.32.180.181 was receiving 403s on POST /mcp to fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com because the per-listener WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites allowed the VPN through the VpnOnly rule but still sent the JSON request body through OWASP managed inspection. AGWFirewallLogs showed SQL injection false positives on ARGS:params.arguments.query with rules 920230, 942100, 942150, 942260, 942360, 942370, 942380, 942410, 942480, then final 949110 anomaly score block. Root cause was that the dedicated site WAF policy copied managed rules but did not include an allow-short-circuit for trusted Rackspace VPN traffic to the MCP endpoint. Fixed live by creating custom allow rule AllowTrustedVpnFazureMcp on listener policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites at priority 3, after RateLimit at 1 and VpnOnly at 2. The rule matches Host fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com and later also fazure-mcp-dev.mscloud.rackspace.com, RequestUri beginning /mcp, and Rackspace VPN IP ranges. This short-circuits m
[specifics: waf_policy: waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites; custom_allow_rule: AllowTrustedVpnFazureMcp (priority=3); rule_priority_order: RateLimit=1, VpnOnly=2, AllowTrustedVpnFazureMcp=3; mcp_hosts: fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com; fazure-mcp-dev.mscloud.rackspace.com; request_uri_prefix: /mcp; rackspace_vpn_ip: 72.32.180.181; owasp_rule_ids: 920230, 942100, 942150, 942260, 942360, 942370, 942380, 942410, 942480, 949110; agw_log_sqli_field: ARGS:params.arguments.query (SQL injection false positives); transaction: d01bb9fed06bbc7fd2c1e28c031119eb (HTTP 200)]", "2026-03-25: On 2026-03-25, persistent 403s from Rackspace VPN IP 72.32.180.181 to fazure-oauth.mscloud.rackspace.com/authorize were traced to a mismatch in the global App Gateway WAF allow rule for MCP dynamic client registration and auth paths. AGWAccessLogs in workspace ecae2296-e4c2-47f2-9336-24599caea6be showed repeated GET /authorize requests returning 403 between 2026-03-25T14:40:58Z and 2026-03-25T14:41:34Z. AGWFirewallLogs showed custom rule BlockKnownPath blocking the full RequestUri including query string, and managed rule 931130 (Possible Remote File Inclusion / Off-Domain Reference/Link) plus 949110 anomaly score also matched on redirect_uri=http://localhost:6274/oauth/callback/debug and resource=https://fazure-mcp-dev.mscloud.rackspace.com/mcp. Root cause was that custom allow rule AllowTrustedVpnFazureOauthMcpAuth only matched bare paths like ^/(.well-known/oauth-authorization-server|register|authorize(?:/callback)?|token|jwks|oauth2/authorize-internal)$, but App Gateway evaluated RequestUri with query string for /authorize. Fixed live by updating the allow rule on global WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax at priority 5 to regex ^/([.]well-known/oauth-authorizat
[specifics: ip: 72.32.180.181; endpoint: fazure-oauth.mscloud.rackspace.com/authorize; workspace_id: ecae2296-e4c2-47f2-9336-24599caea6be; blocked_rule: BlockKnownPath (blocks full RequestUri including query string); managed_rules: 931130, 949110; redirect_uri: http://localhost:6274/oauth/callback/debug; resource: https://fazure-mcp-dev.mscloud.rackspace.com/mcp; allow_rule_before: ^/(.well-known/oauth-authorization-server|register|authorize(?:/callback)?|token|jwks|oauth2/authorize-internal)$; allow_rule_after: ^/([.]well-known/oauth-authorization-server|register|authorize(/callback)?|token|jwks|oauth2/authorize-internal)([?].*)?$; waf_policy: waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax (Allow rule priority=5, BlockKnownPath priority=6)]", "2026-03-25: On 2026-03-25, investigated 403s from Rackspace VPN IP 72.32.180.178 to fazure-oauth.mscloud.rackspace.com/register. Live AGW logs showed OPTIONS /register returned 200, while POST /register was blocked by global WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax with rule 931130 (Possible Remote File Inclusion / Off-Domain Reference/Link) on client_uri=https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/inspector and redirect_uris=http://localhost:6274/oauth/callback/debug, followed by 949110 anomaly score block. Updated global WAF custom rules by moving BlockKnownPath from priority 5 to 6 and adding AllowTrustedVpnFazureOauthMcpAuth at priority 5 for Host=fazure-oauth.mscloud.rackspace.com, RequestUri regex ^/(\.well-known/oauth-authorization-server|register|authorize(?:/callback)?|token|jwks|oauth2/authorize-internal)$, and Rackspace VPN IP ranges. This bypasses managed WAF inspection and the generic authorize/token path blocker for trusted internal MCP OAuth registration/auth flows.
[specifics: client_ip: 72.32.180.178; host: fazure-oauth.mscloud.rackspace.com; waf_policy: waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax; waf_block_rules: 931130, 949110; blocked_request: POST /register blocked; client_uri=https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/inspector; redirect_uris=http://localhost:6274/oauth/callback/debug; options_ok: OPTIONS /register returned 200; BlockKnownPath_priority_change: BlockKnownPath priority 5->6]", "2026-03-19: On 2026-03-19 after expanding the fazure WAF lockdown, a live burst test from Rackspace VPN public IP 72.32.180.181 against https://fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com/mcp did not trigger the Application Gateway WAF RateLimit rule. Baseline responses from the VPN client were /health -> 200 and /mcp -> 401. A 130-request burst to /mcp completed with 130 HTTP 401 responses and the immediate follow-up request also returned 401. Log Analytics confirmed 132 /mcp requests with HttpStatus 401 in the 2026-03-19T19:39:00Z minute for ClientIp 72.32.180.181. AGWFirewallLogs for the same client/host/path only showed Bot Manager matches (rule IDs 300300 and 300700) and no custom rate-limit block events. The WAF policy still shows the RateLimit custom rule enabled at priority 1 with threshold 100, duration OneMin, groupByUserSession ClientAddr, host match values for the four MCP hostnames, RequestUri begins with /mcp, policy mode Prevention, and managed rule set version 3.2 present. This indicates the configured WAF rate-limit rule is still not enforcing in production even after allow-rule reordering.
